# Team Flow — reviewer rules
_Loaded by `flow-reviewer` after Serena activation. Edit freely below the imported block._

# imported from CLAUDE.md (auto-regenerated by /teams:init)
- [must-fix] Enforce coding conventions from CLAUDE.md §Architecture at a glance, §Conventions & gotchas, §Design specs.
# end imported

## Review-phase rules
- [must-fix] Re-run the four exit-gate commands from `project.md` yourself; do not trust the implementer's claims without re-checking. For chord-dice that means `dart format lib test && flutter analyze && flutter test` — if the diff touched any `@riverpod` class, also re-run `dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs` before analyzing.
- [must-fix] Demand a one-line written reason in `phase-N-result.md` for every escape hatch. No reason = `must-fix` issue.
  - Escape-hatch list for this project (language: Dart 3 / Flutter):
    - `dynamic`
    - `late` without justification
    - force-unwrap `!`
- [must-fix] Reject phases where the test count decreased without an explicit Known Delta in the brief.
- [must-fix] Verify dead-code removal across lib/ and test/; a stale symbol is a `must-fix`. Use `find_referencing_symbols` (Serena) to verify no callers remain.
- [must-fix] Honor forbidden-by-invariant rules — flag any violation as `must-fix` regardless of intent. Specifically: any new `AudioService.play*` call from a widget (outside the existing `chord_reference_screen.dart` preview button); any reintroduction of parallel chord-metadata tables instead of `_ChordMeta`; any change to `test/chord_test.dart` motivated by "make this pass"; any hardcoded color literal in a widget instead of `Theme.of(context).colorScheme`; any reintroduction of `(octave + 1) * 12 + semitone` formulas or `% 12` interval math; any branching on `NotationPreference` inside `Chord` / `AudioService` / `noteToMidi`.
- [should-fix] Do not rubber-stamp PASSED. At least one falsifiable check must be executed beyond re-running gates — a Serena `find_referencing_symbols` on a changed symbol, a targeted grep for a forbidden pattern, or a manual re-read of an invariant call site.
- [should-fix] If a pattern looks wrong but is consistent with the rest of the codebase, flag as `should-fix`, not `must-fix`.

## Reviewer-specific tactics
The senior-specialist baseline in `_shared.md` is in force. **Every `[must-fix]` rule there is a `must-fix` review issue here** — speculative generality, defensive validation, WHAT-comments, leftover scaffolding, backwards-compat shims, scope creep, etc. The items below are reviewer-specific framings; they do not duplicate the baseline.

- [must-fix] Distinguish *wrong* from *different from how I'd write it*. Stylistic alternatives are not review issues. Flag a pattern as `must-fix` only when it violates the senior-specialist baseline, a documented invariant, a test, or measurably degrades correctness/clarity.
- [must-fix] Inline-deletion heuristic for new abstractions: ask "what call site would break if I deleted this and inlined it?" If none, the abstraction is premature — `must-fix` per the baseline's YAGNI rule.
- [must-fix] Spec drift check: if the diff touches a subsystem with a named spec under `docs/superpowers/specs/`, verify the spec still describes reality. If the change contradicts the spec without updating it, that is a `must-fix` (either revert the contradiction or update the spec — the planner should have called this out, but the reviewer is the last line).
- [must-fix] Roll-lifecycle check: if `dice_provider.dart` was touched, verify (a) `settleRoll()` remains idempotent, (b) the watchdog timer is still armed in `beginRoll()` and disarmed in `settleRoll()`, (c) `kRollAnimationDuration` / `kRollWatchdogGrace` are still read from `lib/constants.dart` (not inlined), and (d) `current` / `rollingTarget` semantics are preserved (last-settled vs in-flight).
- [should-fix] When proposing a change, name the invariant or measurable consequence it protects. "I'd write it this way" is not a review comment.
